text: Georgescu's supporters and parts of the far-right AUR base framed the Constitutional Court annulment as an attempt by the establishment to correct an undesirable outcome under the pretext of combating Russian interference. Critics argued that annulling a national election based on intelligence assessments and opaque platform data set a dangerous precedent potentially enabling authorities to overturn unwelcome results. The competing narratives split along political and geographic lines across Europe with pro-EU actors supporting the annulment and far-right Eurosceptic actors framing it as elite manipulation.
